summary_one_line: Input must drive an element declaration while the parser is inside the INTERNAL DTD subset (inSubset==1), not the external subset.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML document whose <!ELEMENT ...> declaration is processed while ctxt->inSubset==1, i.e. an element decl placed in the internal DTD subset (inline [...] in the DOCTYPE), so xmlSAX2ElementDecl takes the true branch at SAX2.c:787. The loser inputs only ever reach this function with inSubset==2 (external SYSTEM-loaded subset), taking the false (else-if) branch.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winning value_profile_cmplog seed prepends an extra '%999/x' + reshaped length-prefix header (offsets 0x00-0x0f differ from every loser) and reaches the true branch: in the per-role overlay line 787 is W-only-true (W T=3 F=0) while lines 788-789 (the internal-subset xmlAddElementDecl with intSubset) are [W] only. value_profile adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets that reward partial progress through the chained DOCTYPE/internal-subset structural comparisons, letting vpc assemble an inline internal element declaration that cmplog (edge+I2S only) could not.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every cmplog loser seed shares the identical '....127772.xml' filename header and a 'DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...' body, an EXTERNAL subset reference. In the overlay the loser only reaches line 790 (else if inSubset==2) and the external-subset arm 791-792/804-805 are [L]; line 787 is L T=0 F=41. cmplog gets zero hits on the true branch (avg hitcount on branch: winner=4 loser=0) and stays blocked 23.90h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets, the value_profile delta that distinguishes vpc from cmplog) gives partial-credit coverage for incrementally matching the multi-byte DOCTYPE / internal-subset structural comparisons, steering the corpus toward an inline internal subset so the inSubset==1 true branch fires; plain cmplog has only edge buckets + I2S substitution and never accumulates that gradient.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker's true branch requires the parser to be in the internal subset (inSubset==1).
The winner resolves the branch in 9/10 trials while the loser blocks in all 10, with the winner the only side hitting the true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=4  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving overlay shows the internal-subset arm (intSubset) is W-only.
The loser only reaches the external-subset arm (inSubset==2).
At the blocker line the winner takes the true branch while the loser only takes false.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 787  T=3 F=0  T=0 F=41  if (ctxt->inSubset == 1)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winning seed has a distinct prepended header (%999/x ...) differing from the loser at offsets 0x00 onward.
Every loser seed shares the identical '....127772.xml' filename header.
Loser inputs reference an external SYSTEM subset (DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...).
The distinguishing technique between winner (vpc) and loser (cmplog) is value_profile, which adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive delta for this pair is value_profile.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (the value_profile delta), so I2S lineage verification is not mandatory. Both fuzzers in this pair (cmplog and value_profile_cmplog) carry I2SRandReplace, so I2S cannot be the differentiator; the sole technique vpc adds over cmplog is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback per the MECHANISM CONTEXT section. The dash-row I2S-floor signal would be present for BOTH sides and is therefore non-discriminating here, so no lineage query was run.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on inSubset==1 (internal DTD subset element decl) in which value_profile_cmplog and cmplog reach the true branch at statistically indistinguishable rates would refute the claim that value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient is the enabling technique.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff shows the winner header differs wholesale from the loser but does not directly demonstrate that a CMP_MAP gradient (rather than a lucky structural mutation) was what assembled the inline internal subset.
